scenario.context: Station 02 of the day run: plan modular components.
scenario.mission: You deepen modularization, UML, and clean-code principles for a new component package.

customerRequest.subject: Station 02: Refine module design
customerRequest.body: Please refine the component design, including UML and well-justified structural decisions.

questions.title: Define module boundary
questions.prompt: What should a module boundary be primarily based on?

feedback.correct: Correct: Functional responsibility is the core.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Module boundaries need a functional basis.

questions.title: UML quality
questions.prompt: Which points improve a UML component diagram? (Multiple choice)

options.text: Name interfaces with direction and purpose
Use special notation without a legend

feedback.correct: Correct: This makes the diagram more robust.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please secure interface and dependency transparency more clearly.

questions.title: Design steps
questions.prompt: Put the steps for a clean module design in order.
items: Define the functional responsibility of each module
Review the design with the team
correctOrder: Define the functional responsibility of each module
Review the design with the team

feedback.correct: Correct: The module design is built in a methodical way.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: First define responsibilities and interfaces, then dependencies and review.

questions.title: Review effort
questions.prompt: Three modules are reviewed for 22 minutes each. Another 18 minutes are added for the closing round. What is the total effort?

feedback.wrong: Please add the review times correctly.

questions.title: Justify module design
questions.prompt: Explain in 4-6 sentences why the module split is maintainable.
questions.expectedOutline: Maintainability explained through responsibility boundaries and interface clarity.

feedback.wrong: Please develop responsibility, interface, and maintainability more clearly.
